questionText: Read the following statements and identify which one is correct_______________? Statement 1: Historical elements say how have we reached here. Statement 2: Introduction of the word “competition” demanded other form on innovation
In re-imagining sports through marketing, value of Indian league in 2014- 2022 rose to how many billion dollars?
According to Peter Drucker, a business enterprise has two and only two basic functions. What are they?

Read the following statements and identify which one is correct Statement 1: The basic job of innovation in marketing is to keep the things going with a perspective of benefit realization by both the parties. Statement 2: Product innovation definitely is innovation in marketing
What does the term “Innovation” refer to_________ ?

options.label: A
options.text: A new or improved product or process that differs significantly from previous ones and is actually used or made available to users

options.label: B
options.text: Any routine activity that repeats existing processes without change

options.label: C
options.text: A temporary promotion used only to increase short-term sales

options.label: D
options.text: The simple increase of production volume using the same methods

questionText: In which year did Gary Thuerk, marketing manager at Digital Equipment Corp, sent and emailed back which was considered to be the very first marketing email?
______________means a product’s value proposition gets eroded by the appearance of a number of new products which offer completely different price-benefit positions.
Define the term “commodity trap”

options.label: A
options.text: A phase in which a firm reduces product quality to cut costs but increases prices

options.label: B
options.text: A situation where a company enjoys growing monopoly power and can raise prices freely

options.label: C
options.text: a state when a company sees its competitive position being eroded to an extend that it can no longer command a premium price in its markets.

options.label: D
options.text: A strategy where a firm differentiates its products so strongly that it avoids price competition

questionText: Read the following statements and identify which one is /are correct about Commodity Trap Statement 1: In a commodity trap, the consumer receives more product benefits for their money or pays lower prices for the same or lower benefits. Statement 2: Deterioration creates Commodity trap Statement 3: Understand the deep-seated drivers of perceptions and behavior that are relevant to a product’s appeal is commodity trap. Statement 4: Use a go-to-market strategy appropriate for the innovation and its customer.

options.label: E
options.text: all of the above are correct

questionText: which of the following brand demonstrated innovation in distribution by utilizing the latest technology that made them a trailblazer in the industry.
A product or service becomes “commoditized” when which of the following occurs?

options.label: A
options.text: It enhances operational efficiency so that its cost advantage, rather than differentiation, drives market preference.

options.label: B
options.text: It rapidly lose its intrinsic differentiation value.

options.label: C
options.text: It gradually standardizes its features while maintaining strong, unique brand meanings in consumers’ minds.

options.label: D
options.text: It faces no competition and can charge any price

questionText: When Wal-Mart entered DVD retailing and took share from dominant players like Circuit City and Best Buy by setting prices they could not match profitably, this was an example of which phenomenon?
In the context of the commodity trap, escalation happens when which of the following occurs?

options.label: A
options.text: Companies jointly agree to maintain high prices and avoid competing on benefits

options.label: B
options.text: companies engage in a form of corporate one-upmanship where everyone is so busy trying to outdo each other that the fact that nobody is now making any money escapes attention.

options.label: C
options.text: A market moves from intense rivalry to stable long‑term contracts with fixed profitability

options.label: D
options.text: Firms deliberately reduce product benefits to simplify offerings and increase margins.

questionText: Read the following statements and identify which are correct in relation to consolidation in a market. Statement 1: Consolidation implies that power, strength, or control comes to lie in the hands of a small number of buyers. Statement 2:The process of consolidation is accelerated by increasing mergers, acquisitions, and alliances among previously independent organizations.

options.label: D
options.text: Neither of the statements are correct

questionText: The situation in the retailing industry, where big chains and buying groups gain a massive upper hand over their suppliers, including producers of many branded products, is best described as:
_______________is the study of how customers, both individuals and organizations, satisfy their needs and wants by choosing, purchasing, using and disposing of goods, ideas and services.
_______________generated a value focus by emphasizing innovation while maintaining a clear distinction between ‘renovation’ and ‘innovation’.
The global automobile manufacturing industry, in which suppliers find themselves relying on a shrinking number of Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) customers, is an example of which phenomenon?
Define the term consolidation

options.label: A
options.text: The short‑term promotion technique used to clear excess inventory through discounts

options.label: B
options.text: The strategy of spreading sales evenly across as many small customers as possible in different markets

options.label: C
options.text: The practice of frequently switching suppliers to avoid dependence on any single customer or partner

options.label: D
options.text: The process of growing the concentration of sales and profits among a handful of major customers

questionText: Levi’s, the iconic jeans brand, faced intense competition from many low‑priced rivals, which negatively affected its producer margins and forced the company to introduce the Levi Strauss Signature line at a price about 50% below its traditional jeans in order to stop losing market share. This situation is best described as:

Repositioning by associating a "tainted" product with a category consumers embrace, a company can overcome consumer resistance and advance the product from introduction to growth is called__________________?

In Targeting_______________is satisfied if the targets or customers or segments represent a large enough portion of the market to ensure the profitability of the marketing programs. It is closely connected to the marketing goals and cost structure of the firm in question.

which of the following statements is/are correct in innovation perspective Statement 1: From 2019-2022, the online grocery market in India saw an exponential growth of 56% Compound Annual Growth Rate, according to a study by consulting firm Redseer. Statement 2: Lego is an example of how flexible manufacturing process is used both by the company and customer.

options.label: D
options.text: Neither of the statements are correct

questionText: The gross merchandise value (GMV) refers to_________________?

options.label: A
options.text: The total value of goods sold via e‑commerce platforms over a specific period.

options.label: B
options.text: The net profit remaining after all e‑commerce expenses are deducted

options.label: C
options.text: The total advertising spend of an e‑commerce company in a year

options.label: D
options.text: The average inventory held by an e‑commerce warehouse.

questionText: In the current era of marketing , the traditional STP (Segmentation–Targeting– Positioning) framework is increasingly viewed as shifting toward

options.label: D
options.text: TP - Targeting and Positioning at the individual level

questionText: ________________refers to practices aimed at existing target customers, with existing sales or promotional practices, employing existing arguments and approaches, that has its limits.

__________________requires marketers to develop an enhanced understanding of their industry, including activity at successive steps throughout the value chain, and to apply marketing tools to maximize effectiveness of the marketing programme up and down the value chain.
_______________ is the practice of narrowly focusing marketing activities to attract a specific, clearly defined group of potential customers.

_________________utilized their digital medium to effectively provide highly customized products for their targets. The brand provides online trial for their products to customers by innovation on their platforms.

which of the following statements is/are correct in targeting Statement 1: A service guarantee can be a very good way of persuading customers to buy from you. Statement 2: In responsiveness , segments or customers are actionable if their identification provides guidance for decisions on the effective specification of marketing instruments..

options.label: D
options.text: neither of the statements are correct

questionText: The arrow pointing from "CORE" towards "Targeted Extension" indicates a strategic move that primarily emphasizes:

options.label: B
options.text: Increasing value through segmentation and customization.

options.label: D
options.text: Decreasing value through segmentation and customization.

questionText: A firm develops a package of products and services where the "bundled whole is larger than the sum of its parts." This describes which strategy?
When a brand projects human-like traits, such as Mercedes-Benz suggesting "affluence, prestige and conservatism," which level of brand meaning is being described?
Which of the following lists the five key steps of the "Product to Brands to Passion Brands" pyramid in the correct order, from bottom to top?
The level defined by "Significant similarity, reliability, heritage" comes immediately after which level?
Which differential pricing approach involves using observable buyer characteristics like age, location, or occupation to adjust prices based on an assumed willingness to pay?
When people are given a choice between small, medium, and large portions at a restaurant, they often choose the "middle alternative" (the medium-sized portion). This tendency is called the:
A company gives a 90-day free trial of anti-virus software with a new computer, making the purchaser more likely to buy the software at the end of the trial. This strategy relies on the:
What is "Pay-for-performance pricing"?

options.label: A
options.text: A model where the seller is paid a fixed price, regardless of the outcome.

options.label: B
options.text: A model where the seller is paid depending on performance outcomes determined conjointly with the customer.

options.label: C
options.text: A model where customers pay based on their "willingness to pay."

questionText: In a “Pay What You Want (PWYW)” pricing model, the customer has:

options.label: C
options.text: Full discretion in setting the price

options.label: D
options.text: To pay a fixed minimum price

questionText: Intermediaries, such as wholesalers and retailers, who "buy, take title to, and resell the merchandise" are called:
In banking services, non-linear pricing is observed when:

options.label: A
options.text: Monthly account fees remain the same for all customers

options.label: B
options.text: Monthly account fees drop as customers hold more products

options.label: C
options.text: Interest rates are fixed for all account holders

questionText: The quadrant in the innovation matrix labeled "Missed opportunities" is characterized by:

options.label: A
options.text: High Product Innovation and High Pricing Innovation

options.label: B
options.text: High Product Innovation and Low Pricing Innovation

options.label: C
options.text: Low Product Innovation and High Pricing Innovation

options.label: D
options.text: Low Product Innovation and Low Pricing Innovation

questionText: The pricing strategy where prices "start out at a particular rate and will increase at set intervals of time," such as "early bird offers" for conferences, is known as:

Consider the following statements: Statement 1: Push strategy involves the manufacturer using its salesforce and trade promotion money to induce intermediaries to carry, promote, and sell the product to end users. Statement 2: Pull strategy involves the manufacturer using advertise advertising and promotion to induce customers to ask intermediaries for the product, thus inducing the intermediaries to order it. Which of the following is correct?

options.label: C
options.text: Both Statement 1 and Statement 2 are correct

options.label: D
options.text: Neither Statement 1 nor Statement 2 is correct

questionText: The channel Manufacturer → Wholesaler → Retailer → Consumer represents which type of distribution channel?
A retail store characterized by a "Narrow product line" is known as a:
Telemarketing and online shopping (like Amazon and Flipkart) are examples of which form of nonstore retailing?
Hewlett Packard (HP) uses its sales force, outbound marketing, direct mail, retailers, and the Internet to sell to different customer segments. This is an example of:
Walt Disney’s “My Disney Experience” app, which lets guests plan trips, book dining, check transportation, and shop seamlessly online and in person, is an example of:
When a brand manager converts an idea into an advertisement slogan, this step is called:
The study of signs and symbols and how they convey meaning in communication is called:
The ASICS “Before and After” campaign was designed to emphasize which key idea?

options.label: B
options.text: Mental and emotional benefits of physical activity

questionText: A marketing team places logos on shopping centre floors and uses branded projections on the sides of buildings. This use of "unusual external media to gain impact and publicity" is a media choice known as:
In direct marketing, the absence of marketing middlemen highlights which feature?
When a company uses a call centre to reach potential customers and take orders, it is engaged in:
Which among the following is not a form of non-store retailing?
Nestlé’s “Kit Kat Chunky” campaign used letters that looked like missed delivery notes. This is an example of which form of marketing?
Decathlon’s use of RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) technology to track inventory and assist checkout primarily enhances which component of the marketing mix?
The effectiveness of a creative message in marketing communication largely depends on:

options.label: C
options.text: The choice of methods and media used to deliver it

options.label: D
options.text: The production cost of the advertisement

questionText: Which of the following are major tools used in consumer sales promotion?
_____________________ is the process of dealing with legislators and government officials to promote or defeat legislation and regulations.

As a part of spreading their commitment to sustainability, Patagonia launched a campaign in 2011, by featuring a full-page ad in The New York Times, showing the picture of their best-selling jacket with the campaign tagline ___________________.

According to American Marketing Association, _________________ is the placement of announcements and messages in time or space by business firms, nonprofit organizations, government agencies, and individuals who seek to inform and/or persuade members of a particular target market or audience regarding their products, services, organizations or ideas.
What does SEO stand for in the context of Digital Marketing?
Name the campaign launched by Spotify to showcase their ability to target consumers effectively and, in turn, helped in their year-over-year growth.
Which of the following best describes Affiliate Marketing?

options.label: A
options.text: It is a performance-based exercise that enables revenue sharing and pay-per- sale (PPS) compensation within a common network.

options.label: B
options.text: It refers to the publishing and distribution of text, video or audio materials to customers online.

options.label: C
options.text: It involves blending marketing materials into a medium, making the underlying message and marketing purposes equally important.

options.label: D
options.text: It is an online method for advertising where a business only pays for its ads when a person clicks on them.

questionText: _________________ is the association of all the stakeholders involved rather than just creating a product.
Which ITC brand launched the “Har Dil Ki Fantasy” campaign featuring Shah Rukh Khan, utilizing Artificial Intelligence and social media for their promotion?
_____________ is an online method for advertising where a business only pays for its ads when a person clicks on them.
___________ is a public service, non-profit organization seeking to provide solutions, services, and security to millions of India's rural migrant workers.
Which of the following statements about Airbnb and its influence on the hospitality sector is true?

options.label: A
options.text: Airbnb has encouraged hotels to introduce greater customization and personalized guest experiences instead of relying only on standard operating procedures.

options.label: B
options.text: Airbnb has inspired new hotel concepts that emphasize unique design and high-quality food & beverage offerings, especially in budget hotels.

options.label: C
options.text: Airbnb has led most hotels to eliminate personalization and return strictly to rigid standardized services

options.label: D
options.text: Airbnb’s influence includes the use of repurposed or existing buildings where the original function becomes part of the guest experience.

questionText: ___________________ is part of the Diffusion phase of the innovation value chain.
In the Innovation value chain, idea creation within a unit is known as ____________, while collaboration across unit is known as _____________________.
Which AI-powered virtual assistant is integrated into the Amazon Echo, a smart device introduced in 2014 by Amazon?

A customer books a hotel room and asks for a room with free Wi-Fi. They also expect the room to be clean and comfortable, even though they did not explicitly mention it. In this situation, the customer’s expectation that the room will be clean and comfortable is an example of which type of need?

A mechanical engineering professor from the University of Canterbury spent about 15 years researching and developing a safer trampoline design for his daughter. His invention, the Springfree Trampoline, uses flexible composite rods instead of traditional metal springs to reduce the risk of injuries. Who is this inventor?

Around 2010, insurance data in Sweden revealed that cyclists accounted for a higher proportion of road casualties than any other type of road user. This highlighted an unmet safety need, prompting Volvo to introduce innovations such as bike-detection sensors, automatic braking, and driver-alert systems. What was the unmet need identified by Volvo from this data?

options.label: B
options.text: Enhancing safety for cyclists and other road users on the road

options.label: C
options.text: Increasing the speed and performance of cars

options.label: D
options.text: Reducing the cost of vehicle manufacturing

questionText: Which company specializes in developing financial and accounting software for individuals and businesses, including products such as Quicken and QuickBooks?
Which of the following statements are true about Carvaan?

options.label: A
options.text: It is a portable digital music player that has the appearance and feel of a vintage transistor radio but performs modern music player functions.

options.label: B
options.text: The majority of customers buy a Carvaan unit in order to access the 5000 tracks that were handpicked after thorough research.

options.label: C
options.text: Carvaan consistently conveys through its campaigns the importance of entertainment in the lives of the elderly and emphasizes the profound impact music can have on their ability to navigate through challenging circumstances.

options.label: D
options.text: It does not contain any pre-loaded songs and hence require internet connection to work properly.

questionText: Which marketing concept, as described by Beth Comstock (Vice Chair of GE), refers to an approach that continuously connects a company’s offerings to markets and generates new energy around invention, particularly in innovation- driven organizations?
Which Dabur-owned fruit juice brand successfully tackled initial consumer taste perception challenges by emphasizing natural ingredients and health benefits to establish itself in the Indian market?
Nirmai’s developing Thermalytix, Samsungs new foldable smartphones and Mitticool’s clay refrigerators are all examples marketing innovations through _______.

____________was initially developed as a refrigerant and as a coating to prevent corrosion on electrical wiring and was later repurposed and became most famous as a non-stick coating for cookware. It is an example of marketing of innovation through new usage.
Who stated that “Thinking like a designer can transform the way you develop products, services, processes – and even strategy”?
Which of the following elements is a part of the GE formula for successful marketing of innovations?
Marketing of innovation through a new perspective entails altering how consumers view a brand or product. Which of the following is an example of the same?

options.label: A
options.text: Dove challenged traditional beauty standards by promoting real women with diverse body shapes and skin types, encouraging self-acceptance and self- esteem.

options.label: B
options.text: Spotify disrupted the music industry by offering a vast library of songs that users can stream for free or with premium subscriptions, making music more accessible and convenient.

options.label: C
options.text: Samsung introduced foldable smartphones, which offer a new form factor by allowing users to unfold the phone into a larger tablet-like screen.

options.label: D
options.text: Niramai developed Thermalytix, an innovative breast cancer screening solution that uses thermography and artificial intelligence to detect breast cancer at an early stage.

questionText: Who invented the shopping cart in the 1930s to make shopping more convenient for customers and increase grocery store sales?
According to research by Abbie Griffin and colleagues, which of the following themes was identified as requiring special attention to strengthen the intersection between marketing and innovation?

options.label: A
options.text: Improving customer needs understanding and customer involvement in developing new products.

options.label: B
options.text: Eliminating the role of marketing in innovation processes.

options.label: C
options.text: Disseminating and implementing academic research within firms

questionText: Which early e-reader device, despite being technologically advanced, struggled in the market partly because it lacked a well-developed ecosystem of publishing partners and content support?
What characteristic is emphasized as essential for an effective innovation?

options.label: A
options.text: It should combine several complex functions to maximize appeal

options.label: B
options.text: It must be technologically advanced, regardless of user understanding

options.label: C
options.text: It should be simple and focused on doing one thing well

options.label: D
options.text: It should target as many user segments as possible

questionText: Which product range was part of Pampers' shift toward developmental segmentation in its long-term strategy?

options.label: A
options.text: DryMax SuperFit, designed for overnight use

options.label: C
options.text: UltraFlex Mobility, aimed at toddlers aged 2–5

options.label: D
options.text: Infant Comfort Series, targeting sensitive skin

questionText: Dr. Reddy’s cost-efficient production methods primarily support which strategic advantage?

options.label: A
options.text: Reducing dependence on international regulatory approvals

options.label: B
options.text: Limiting the need for technological collaborations

options.label: C
options.text: Avoiding investments in drug delivery innovations

options.label: D
options.text: Delivering high-quality pharmaceuticals at competitive prices

questionText: Which of the following best reflects Dr. Reddy’s use of partnerships in its innovation strategy?

options.label: A
options.text: Collaborations that focus exclusively on marketing and distribution within India

options.label: B
options.text: Partnerships intended mainly to outsource production to reduce costs

options.label: C
options.text: Alliances with global pharmaceutical companies to access advanced technologies and expand the product portfolio

options.label: D
options.text: Joint ventures focused solely on generic drug price negotiations

questionText: Which of the following best reflects the primary starting point for crafting an effective innovation strategy?

options.label: A
options.text: Identifying emerging technologies and adopting them early

options.label: B
options.text: Benchmarking the innovation practices of industry leaders

options.label: C
options.text: Building financial forecasts for innovation investments

options.label: D
options.text: Clearly articulating objectives that support sustainable competitive advantage

questionText: Which of the following are NOT a source of innovation found within a company?
Vic Mills’ observation in the 1950s that led to the creation of Pampers primarily highlighted which critical gap in existing baby care solutions?

options.label: C
options.text: Poor pricing strategies in the baby care market

questionText: Why is relying solely on intellectual property typically insufficient for companies to capture value from innovation?

options.label: A
options.text: Because patents expire faster than they used to

options.label: C
options.text: Because imitators often enter the market quickly, intensifying price competition

options.label: D
options.text: Because IP laws prevent cross-industry diffusion of technologies

questionText: What major technological advancement in the 1940s significantly accelerated the development of synthetic diamonds?

options.label: C
options.text: Percy Williams Bridgman's high-pressure apparatus enabling greater material compression

options.label: D
options.text: Development of AI-driven gemstone imaging systems

questionText: According to the Innovation Landscape Map, which type of innovation requires a new business model but leverages existing technical competences?
Which of the following reflect how consumers apply the endowment effect when assessing innovations?

options.label: A
options.text: They view benefits of current products as part of their psychological ownership

options.label: B
options.text: They compare gains and losses relative to their existing products

options.label: C
options.text: They perceive switching to new products as involving significant losses

options.label: D
options.text: They evaluate innovations objectively, independent of prior product use

questionText: Which statement best captures the role of innovation resistance in the adoption process?

options.label: A
options.text: All adopter groups experience equal resistance to innovations

options.label: B
options.text: Resistance determines whether an innovation is eventually rejected entirely

options.label: C
options.text: Differences in resistance among adopter categories affect the timing of adoption

options.label: D
options.text: Only laggards display resistance, while all other groups show immediate acceptance

questionText: Which of the following best represents the central responsibility of entrepreneurial leaders during the Explore stage of the innovation lifecycle?

options.label: A
options.text: Scaling the business using established growth options

options.label: B
options.text: Achieving traction with early adopters and early majority customers

options.label: C
options.text: Clarifying assumptions, identifying uncertainties, and selecting an opportunity

options.label: D
options.text: Designing MVP-based experiments to test market entry

questionText: Which of the following are Key Activities in the Experiment stage? (Select ALL that apply.)

options.label: A
options.text: Develop market entry solutions using MVP

options.label: C
options.text: Analyse personal motivations and build the founding team

options.label: D
options.text: Refine the business model and manage risk

questionText: Which of the following best explains the key risk of using an existing brand name for an innovative product?

options.label: A
options.text: It guarantees lower advertising expenses, leading to weaker market presence

options.label: B
options.text: It increases the likelihood that competitors will imitate the product

options.label: C
options.text: A failed extension can confuse consumers and damage the parent brand

options.label: D
options.text: It eliminates the possibility of launching a sub-brand in the future

questionText: For companies with limited advertising resources, which approach is generally recommended?

options.label: A
options.text: Launching new brands to appear more innovative and visible.

options.label: B
options.text: Expanding product categories broadly under one brand.

options.label: C
options.text: Avoiding the process of creating a brand-new name

options.label: D
options.text: Prioritizing sub-branding to replace direct extensions

questionText: Which strategic move most contributed to Spotify’s ability to differentiate itself during its early expansion phase?

options.label: A
options.text: Launching exclusively in the U.S. market before Europe

options.label: B
options.text: Offering a premium-only subscription model with no free tier

options.label: C
options.text: Securing licensing deals with major record labels to build a comprehensive catalogue

options.label: D
options.text: Relying solely on user-generated music uploads

questionText: Which of the following best explains why Microsoft's traditional “seat license” model created the “thank you and see you in three years” sales culture?

options.label: A
options.text: Salespeople had limited access to enterprise clients after initial onboarding

options.label: B
options.text: Contracts were short-term, requiring constant renewals

options.label: C
options.text: Sales commissions were tied to closing contracts rather than continuous customer engagement

options.label: D
options.text: Customers preferred yearly negotiations over long-term contracts

questionText: Which of the following best defines the "Innovation Lifecycle"?

options.label: A
options.text: A linear process where innovation moves directly from idea generation to market success.

options.label: B
options.text: A structured approach where all innovations must reach the maturity phase before decline.

options.label: C
options.text: A system where innovations must always disrupt an existing market to be successful.

options.label: D
options.text: A cycle where companies explore, experiment, emerge, exploit, and transform over time.

questionText: Which of the following is NOT one of the three branding strategies for new products?
Which factor most directly contributed to the Segway PT being perceived as a luxury rather than a practical transportation device?

options.label: A
options.text: Its use of advanced balancing technology

options.label: B
options.text: Its marketing emphasis on environmental sustainability

options.label: C
options.text: Its high price, which made it inaccessible to a broad consumer base

questionText: A company with strong intellectual-property assets, limited desire to invest in manufacturing, and entry into a market where extensive infrastructure is needed should choose which innovation approach?
According to Peter Drucker, innovation primarily aims to:

options.label: A
options.text: Maintain stability within an enterprise while minimizing change

options.label: C
options.text: Create intentional change that enhances an enterprise’s economic or social potential

options.label: D
options.text: Replace existing business functions with automated systems

questionText: What was a significant insight from Pampers’ late-1990s research regarding its competition with Huggies?

options.label: A
options.text: Pampers needed to focus on reducing manufacturing cost to compete

options.label: B
options.text: Improving core functionality alone would not significantly increase market share

options.label: C
options.text: Consumers preferred Huggies due to lower prices.

options.label: D
options.text: Pampers lacked adequate global distribution capabilities

questionText: Which strategic choice by Dr. Reddy’s Laboratories most strongly demonstrates its long-term commitment to innovation leadership?

options.label: A
options.text: Prioritizing marketing expenditure over research to strengthen brand recall

options.label: B
options.text: Reducing R&D allocation to focus on low-cost manufacturing

options.label: C
options.text: Sustained investment in basic research and R&D despite opposing industry trends

options.label: D
options.text: Limiting product development to only existing markets

questionText: Toyota’s approach with the Prius illustrates which strategy for minimizing resistance?
What psychological bias leads consumers to Favor products they already own over new innovations?
Which of the following initiatives demonstrate Taco Bell’s repositioning effort toward a more contemporary and immersive customer experience? (Select all that apply.)

options.label: A
options.text: Opening upscale Taco Bell Cantina locations with urban design elements

options.label: B
options.text: Introducing a breakfast menu to extend operating hours

options.label: C
options.text: Subtly refreshing branding while retaining the iconic bell logo

options.label: D
options.text: Maintaining the long-running “Yo Quiero Taco Bell” mascot campaign

questionText: Which component of the four-part framework for breaking into networked markets focuses on aligning with entities that hold influence over adoption?

options.label: D
options.text: Reason back from a target endgame

questionText: According to David Robertson's "Power of Little Ideas," what characterizes the Third Way approach to innovation?

options.label: A
options.text: Introducing radical breakthroughs that immediately replace existing products

options.label: B
options.text: Competing in low-end markets using a cost-driven business model

options.label: C
options.text: Developing coordinated complementary innovations surrounding a central product

options.label: D
options.text: Creating entirely new markets by targeting previously ignored customer groups
